page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-12xCPU-24GB is an x86_64 architecture virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. It features 12 vCPUs with shared CPU allocation, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 24.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this server type, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates that single-core performance is a primary tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring 784.69, placing it in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 9386.85 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8209.0. Memory bandwidth is consistent, measuring 1218.59 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 60.68 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

Given the shared CPU allocation and 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, this server offers a cost-efficient option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated single-core performance. It is suitable for tasks such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 9.63 GB/sec, or multi-threaded decompression. It is less suitable for resource-intensive tasks like medium-parameter LLM text generation, where it exhibits weak performance of 6.21 tokens/sec.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-12xCPU-24GB is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 12 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz and 24.0 GB of RAM. This server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a notable tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, scoring in the bottom 10% on stress-ng, whereas multi-core and memory benchmarks perform at average levels. Specifically, the server achieves a Geekbench multi-core score of 8209.0 and a static web serving throughput of 9.63 GB/sec. Given its 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio and shared CPU allocation, this configuration provides a cost-efficient option for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and basic database operations, while remaining less suitable for single-threaded tasks or intensive LLM text generation.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-12xCPU-24GB is an x86_64 server with 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 24.0 GB RAM. It offers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core benchmark results.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-12xCPU-24GB is an x86_64 server featuring 12 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 24.0 GB RAM, suitable for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A shared-core virtual server featuring twelve AMD EPYC vCPUs and a compute-aligned memory ratio for multi-threaded workloads.
